title: anguina dioica
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", anguina dioica (Bryonia dioica) benefits include exerting an important cytotoxic effect against human carcinoma cells and suppressing vascularization in vivo.
parts: Anguina dioica has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and seeds, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds. The roots of Anguina dioica are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant contain saponins, such as angustifolin and dioscin, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The seeds of Anguina dioica contain essential oils, including terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive problems.
side_effects: Anguina dioica can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Improper use of Anguina dioica can also lead to an increased heart rate, anxiety, and tremors in some individuals. Furthermore, excessive consumption can cause liver and kidney damage, making it essential to use the plant under the guidance of a medical professional.
